topic: What does a sync placement actually pay?
hook: A South African composer just earned more from one Showmax documentary than from 6 months of streaming royalties.
cta: Save this. Then ask yourself: is your music sync-ready?

caption_full:

This is not exceptional.

Save this. Then ask yourself: is your music sync-ready?

#SyncMaster #AfricanComposers #SyncLicensing #MusicBusiness #StreamingVsSync

slides.headline: A South African composer just earned more from one

slides.body:
This is not exceptional. This is the market rate. ↓ 🎮 AAA video game
$5,000 – $20,000+ These are upfront licensing fees.
Not royalties. Not splits. Not "exposure."
Here's what sync placements actually pay: You also earn performance royalties every time the content airs.

📺 TV (major network, 30 sec)
$2,000 – $8,000 Streaming: fractions of a cent per play.
Sync: thousands, once, yours.

🎬 Feature film (indie)
$5,000 – $15,000+ The market exists. The demand is real.
What's missing is the infrastructure connecting African composers to it.

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.body: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
